summary: High‑energy show with soaring Dark Star > Terrapin and a monster Deal
blurb: The second set delivers a dramatic Dark Star into Terrapin Station marathon, highlighted by an incendiary Loose Lucy and a powerful Deal. Jerry’s guitar is on fire throughout, while Hornsby’s keyboards add extra flavor despite occasional mix forwardness. Overall the show ranks among the strongest of 1990.
review: On December 12, 1990 at McNichols Sports Arena the Dead unleashed one of their most electrifying Colorado outings. The first set rolls through a solid run of classics—Touch of Grey, Greatest Story Ever Told and Candyman—before a loose‑loving Loose Lucy that hints at the fireworks to come. The real high point arrives in the second set: a seamless Dark Star > Terrapin Station journey that builds from an inspired China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider segue into an expansive Dark Star, then rockets through Iko Iko and a thunderous Deal. Jerry Garcia’s lead guitar is described as “incendiary,” “soaring,” and “gritty” across recordings, delivering some of his most adventurous improvisation of the year. Bruce Hornsby’s keyboards add bright Cajun‑flavored texture—though a few listeners note the mix can be forward—but many praise its tight integration with Garcia’s lead lines. The twin drummers, Mickey Hart and Bill Kreutzmann, provide powerful thunder that drives the second‑set transitions, especially during the climactic Drum/Space segment leading into All Along the Watchtower and a soul‑stirring Stella Blue. With multiple soundboard sources confirming crystal‑clear capture of these moments, this concert stands out as a must‑listen snapshot of the Dead’s 1990 brilliance.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Six of seven recordings are overwhelmingly positive, repeatedly praising Dark Star, Terrapin Station, Loose Lucy, Deal and the second‑set flow. No consistent negative comments about performance appear; only one mixed review notes a rough first set but still highlights the strong second half. This strong consensus yields a high positive sentiment adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Good soundboard source with crisp instrument separation, captures the full second‑set marathon and balances mix despite occasional front‑heavy moments.

key_highlights: Dark Star > Terrapin Station marathon in the second set
Incendiary Loose Lucy and monster Deal showcase Garcia’s peak playing
Bruce Hornsby’s keyboard adds bright texture, though sometimes forward in the mix

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia delivers incendiary, soaring lead guitar throughout—trippy noodling on Dark Star, gritty licks on Loose Lucy and a monster Deal that fans call ‘on fire.’
band_performance.Phil: Provides a solid bass foundation and steps up for lead vocals on “The Deal,” adding depth to the groove.
band_performance.Bruce Hornsby: Hornsby’s keyboards add bright, Cajun‑flavored color; many reviewers praise his dynamic interplay with Garcia while noting occasional forward mix balance issues.
band_performance.Vince Welnick: His synth/keyboard work dominates tracks like Mexicali Blues and Throwing Stones, earning strong positive remarks for texture.
band_performance.Mickey: Hart’s percussion delivers powerful thunder, especially in the second‑set transitions and Drum/Space segment.
band_performance.Billy: Kreutzmann matches Hart with tight, propulsive drumming that drives the jams forward.
